title: Benchmark run manifest (Contract 3)
description: The full configuration manifest of one benchmark run. Normative. Every field the benchmark comparability rule (compatibility-policy.md §7) keys on is required and explicit: results are comparable only when model revision, quantization, tokenizer, engine version+flags, hardware, driver/CUDA, workload version+seed, and warm-up policy all match, or the difference is the single declared experimental variable. A manifest missing any of these is not a valid run record.

run_id.description: Unique run identifier; raw events and results link back to it.

target_topology.description: What the load generator talks to: the engine directly, the gateway in front of a real engine, or the gateway in front of the mock backend. via-gateway and gateway-mock REQUIRE the gateway block; engine-direct MUST NOT carry one.

workload_ref.description: Exact workload identity this run executed (comparability keys on workload version + seed).

commit.description: Source commit of the engine build; null only when genuinely unavailable (must then be noted in the result's validity block).

flags.description: ALL non-default runtime flags of the engine process. Named properties below are typed for the common engines (as of 2026-07 — flag names are engine-specific and versioned; re-verify at use time); any other flag is recorded as an additional key. An empty object asserts the engine ran with pure defaults.

quantization.description: Quantization method active for this run (e.g. awq, gptq, fp8, gguf-q4_k_m); null = none.

speculative_decoding.description: Speculative decoding configuration verbatim; null = disabled.

checkpoint.description: Model checkpoint identity (e.g. HF repo id or artifact path).

tokenizer.description: Tokenizer identity used for all token counts in this run.

hardware.description: Null values are permitted only where the field is genuinely not applicable (e.g. CPU-only mock runs) and assert 'not applicable', never 'not recorded'.

instance_type.description: Cloud instance type or a self-describing local machine label.

config_version.description: Gateway config snapshot version active during the run (matches the inference.config_version trace attribute).

location.description: Where the load generator ran relative to the target (e.g. same-host, same-zone, region name).

rtt_ms.description: Measured network round-trip time from client to target; null only for same-process mocks.

warm_up.description: Warm-up policy. Comparability keys on it; the result's validity block records how warm-up was handled in aggregation.

value.description: Seconds for discard-duration, request count for discard-requests.

repetitions.description: Number of repetitions of the workload in this run set.

hypothesis.description: The falsifiable statement this run tests, including the single declared experimental variable if the run is part of a comparison.

contracts_bundle_version.description: serving-contracts bundle tag the emitting tool was pinned to.
